changes: Added std::process::Command import for shell spawning
Added subshell_worktree: Option<PathBuf> field to App struct for storing pending subshell path
Implemented prepare_subshell() method with two-tier worktree lookup: meta.json workspace_path first, then .bop/workspaces/<id>/ fallback
Implemented run_subshell() method that spawns $SHELL in worktree with stderr user guidance messages
Both methods properly handle missing cards and worktrees with status messages
Added KeyCode::Char('!') handler in handle_normal() that calls prepare_subshell()
Updated test helper struct to initialize subshell_worktree: None
Added crossterm imports for cursor, raw mode control, and screen mode (LeaveAlternateScreen, EnterAlternateScreen)
Implemented subshell execution block in event loop: check mode == Mode::Subshell, leave alt screen, disable raw mode, run_subshell(), restore terminal state, clear for redraw

patterns_discovered.description: Key handler (prepare_subshell) is called during event dispatch, sets mode and stores path. Event loop checks mode, manages terminal state, then executes run_subshell. This decouples key input from shell execution.
Tries meta.json workspace_path field first, then falls back to .bop/workspaces/<id>/ relative to project root. Validates each path with is_dir() before accepting.

Before spawning subshell: cursor::Show, LeaveAlternateScreen, disable_raw_mode. After shell exits: enable_raw_mode, EnterAlternateScreen, cursor::Hide, terminal.clear(). Ensures TUI can resume cleanly.
Status messages in footer for errors (no card, no worktree). Stderr messages inform user of directory and how to return to bop ui.

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Terminal state must be fully restored after subshell
gotchas_discovered.impact: Failure to leave alt screen or disable raw mode before spawning shell causes terminal corruption when user types
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Implementation properly pairs LeaveAlternateScreen/disable_raw_mode before, enable_raw_mode/EnterAlternateScreen/cursor::Hide after

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: $SHELL env var may not exist on all systems
gotchas_discovered.impact: Subshell spawn fails if SHELL unset and no fallback provided
Code could attempt to spawn shell in non-existent directory, failing silently
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Both lookup paths validated with is_dir() before setting subshell_worktree

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Mode state mutation during event loop
gotchas_discovered.impact: If mode is set to Subshell but subshell_worktree is None, run_subshell() silently resets mode
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: prepare_subshell() ensures both are set together, run_subshell() takes ownership with .take()

discoveries.approach_outcome: SUCCESS - Vim :! convention fully implemented with proper terminal lifecycle management. Key press triggers prepare_subshell() which validates worktree and sets mode. Event loop detects mode, suspends TUI (leaves alt screen, disables raw mode), executes run_subshell() synchronously, restores TUI state, and forces redraw. Comprehensive error handling for missing cards/worktrees via status messages. User experience includes stderr guidance text showing directory and exit instructions.

recommendations.recommendation: Add help text to footer or F-key legend indicating ! key opens subshell (similar to how Ctrl+O is documented in mc pattern)
Test with $SHELL pointing to zsh, fish, nushell, and various non-standard shells to verify fallback and path expansion works
Consider tracking shell session history per card in meta.json or .bop/workspaces/<id>/shell_history for future audit/replay
For 'no worktree' case, consider suggesting which cards have workspaces via status message (e.g., 'no worktree for card-123, try J to move')
Update bop ui keybinding docs to include ! → subshell mapping alongside existing Tab multi-select, Shift+H/L move, Ctrl+O patterns
